title: Lifespan Respite Care Reauthorization Act of 2025
short_title: Lifespan Respite Care Reauthorization Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill gives money to programs that help families who take care of sick or disabled family members. These programs provide temporary breaks so caregivers can rest or do other things they need to do. The bill also changes the rules so that kids under 18 who help take care of family members can get help too, not just adults. The money will keep these important programs running so families can get the support they need.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions. (Sponsor introductory remarks on measure: CR S1498)

sections.title: Section 1. Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Lifespan Respite Care Reauthorization Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2. Reauthorization of Lifespan Respite Care Program
sections.text: This section makes two changes to an existing law called the Public Health Service Act. First, it changes the definition of family caregiver. The old law said a family caregiver had to be an unpaid adult. The new law says a family caregiver can be any unpaid individual, which means it now includes people under eighteen years old. Second, it extends the funding for the lifespan respite care program. The old law provided money for the years two thousand twenty through two thousand twenty four. The new law will provide money for the years two thousand twenty five through two thousand thirty.
